records.ety: [OE. credo, crede, AS. creda, fr. L. credo I believe, at the beginning of the Apostles' creed, fr. credere to believe; akin to OIr. cretim I believe, and Skr. çraddadhāmi; çrat trust + dhā to put. See Do, v. t., and cf. Credo, Grant.]

defs: A definite summary of what is believed; esp., a summary of the articles of Christian faith; a confession of faith for public use; esp., one which is brief and comprehensive.
quotes: In the Protestant system the creed is not coordinate with, but always subordinate to, the Bible.

defs: Any summary of principles or opinions professed or adhered to.
quotes: I love him not, nor fear him; there's my creed.
That part which is so creeded by the people.
